content.title: The True Home of the Heart
content.subtitle: May we return to the One who sees every hidden tear.
content.body:
Have you ever said to Him, "Ya Rabb, I love You"?

We are a people who deeply crave love, who ache to be loved. Our hearts are filled with countless untold stories, with emotions left unspoken that weigh like stones upon the left side of our chest. Hoping for just a little sincere attention, longing for even a trace of affection, we spend our lives running after people. We stay awake night after night, waiting for a simple reply from someone on the other end of the phone, breathing countless sighs in the process.

We love to talk. We long for someone dear with whom we can stand on a rooftop at midnight or lean against a window grille and pour out every thought hidden behind the walls of our hearts. We desperately seek someone to whom we can entrust our sorrows, our cherished memories, our joys, our disappointments, and the endless garlands of words woven from our deepest emotions.

2. We Forget the Heart's True Destination

But have we ever stopped to think about this? The Lord who placed all this love within our hearts... have we ever shared these stored-up stories with Him?

3. The One Who Knows Every Hidden Part of Us

The One who clearly hears the silent sigh buried deep within our chest, the one that never even reached our lips...

The One who sees the tears we desperately try to hide from the world by burying our faces into our pillows in the stillness of the night… Have we ever told Him the secrets of this restless heart?

4. The Loving Call in the Last Part of the Night

We willingly spend sleepless nights weaving stories of love with people.

Yet during the stillness of the last part of the night, when our Lord descends, in a manner befitting His Majesty, to the lowest heaven and lovingly calls out: "Who is there to ask of Me, so that I may give to them?"

We remain fast asleep. We search throughout the world for someone to mend our broken hearts, while the One waiting to heal every wound with His infinite mercy patiently awaits us.

Yet somehow, we never seem to find the time to shed even a couple of tears before Him.

5. Shall We Talk to Our Lord Tonight?

Come. When the noise fades away tonight...When the world falls asleep… Let us spend a little time talking to our Lord. Let us quietly pour before Him every untold story of our lives, every hidden longing, every ache carried by this love-starved heart. And let us whisper to Him, just once… "Ya Rabb... I love You so very much."
